publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-06-21 00:04:51 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Film, videos, and other material relating to the Cornell University ice hockey teams.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Films of Cornell University hockey games. Some films in color. Also three hockey instructional movies. Films vary in type; some have sound and some do not; some are black and white and some are color. Also a DVD with some news clippings. Audio cassettes of games and ceremonies recorded by Fleet Morse. Hockey Puck, from February 26, 1938 game at West Point, Army 2 - Cornell 1. Also DVDs of broadcasts of game films. Also includes hockey awards banquet and game programs, including the January 6, 1973 Cornell/Harvard program (the game in which the famous chicken was tied to the goal post) and assorted clippings. "Annual College Hockey Tournament, Olympic Arena, Lake Placid" program, n.d..

Letter (verifax copy) from Barnaby Conrad Keeney, President of Brown University, in a program for the 18th NCAA hockey championships, 1965, held at Brown University, describing the birth of American college hockey and mentioning that among the first hockey players in the United States was a Cornellian, William A. Larned (Class of 1893).
Digitization of films can be arranged if requested.

odd.head: General
odd.p: See also collection #3-13-2843 for a VHS tape of the Cornell Hockey team performing the Nutcracker on ice, October 26, 1992, this might actually be Swan Lake. Stills from this performance can be found in the University Photo collection, #4-14-2883, Job 978/92 in Boxes 11, 13, and 28.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Portions of other Cornell films can be viewed in the video available at http://www.lynahrink.com

odd.head: General
odd.p: Digitization of films can be arranged if requested.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Ice Hockey collection, #40-4-3590. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions and Permissions page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many programs have completed score sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many programs have completed score sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete run. Contains December 2; January 3; March 5 (ECAC first round program at Lynah Rink.) For more programs, see Mintz addition, box 13 folder 11.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete run. Contains November 23; January 8; February 15, 18; March 1, 4. Includes March 7-8 ECAC championship program. See Mintz addition, box 13 folder 12, for the December 7 program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes December 22-23 ECAC Holiday Hockey Festival program, January 2-3 Syracuse Invitational Tournament program, March 13-14 ECAC Championship program, and March 19-21 NCAA Hockey program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes 1971 NCAA Championship program, March 9 ECAC first round program, and March 12-13 ECAC Championship program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes December 29-30 Syracuse Invitational Tournament program, March 7 ECAC first round program, March 10-11 ECAC Championship program, and March 16-18 NCAA Championship program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes December 29-30 Syracuse Invitational Tournament program, March 9-10 ECAC Championship program, and March 15-17 NCAA Championship program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes scrimmage program, December 5 St. Lawrence University program, December 28-29 Syracuse Invitational Tournament program, March 5 ECAC Quarter-finals program, and March 8-9 ECAC Championship program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes scrimmage program, December 27-28 Syracuse Invitational Tournament program, January 3-5 ECAC Holiday Hockey Festival program, and March 4 ECAC Quarter-finals program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes scrimmage program, December 26-27 Syracuse Invitational Tournament program, and March 12-13 ECAC Championship program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes scrimmage program, December 28-29 Syracuse Invitational program, March 8 ECAC Quarter-final program, March 11-12 ECAC Championship program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes scrimmage program and March 7 ECAC Quarter-final program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes scrimmage program and March 6 ECAC Quarterfinal program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes February 13 RPI program, March 27-29 NCAA Hockey Championship program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes February 21 RPI program and March 10 ECAC Quarterfinal program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes February 6 RPI program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete run. Contains November 20-21; December 4; January 18, 22, 24; February 4, 5, 18, 25-27. For December 12 program, see Mintz addition box 13 folder 28.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes March 8-9 ECAC Quarterfinal program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes March 7-8 ECAC Quarterfinal program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes March 4-5 ECAC Quarterfinal program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes Syracuse Invitational Tournament program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes March 2-3 ECAC quarterfinals program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes Syracuse Invitational Tournament program and March 1-2 ECAC quarterfinals program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete run. Missing November 22-23. Includes Cleveland College Hockey Classic tournament program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes ECAC championships program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes ECAC program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes ECAC program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes exhibition game programs, Syracuse Invitational Tournament program, and ECAC Quarterfinal program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete run. Missing programs for January 30, 31, and February 27, 28.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run.

scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes ECAC First Round program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes Syracuse Invitational Tournament program and ECAC First Round program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete run. Missing program for February 1. Includes ECAC Quarterfinal program.

scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes ECAC Quarterfinal program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes Florida College Hockey Classic program,Colgate program and ECAC Quarterfinal program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes Brown program, Yale program, Florida College Hockey Classic, Colgate program, and ECAC Quarterfinal program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete run. Missing programs for November 2, 3. Includes Princeton program, RIT program, Madison Square Garden game against Boston program, Florida College Hockey Classic program, and ECAC First Round program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete run. Includes ECAC Quarterfinal program and ECAC Championship program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete run. Missing programs for October 23, 24, 30; November20, 21, 24; January 22, 23, 29, 30; February 12, 13. Includes Madison Square Garden game against Boston program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete run. Missing programs for December 3, 4; January 14, 15; February 18, 19. Includes Florida College Hockey Classic program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.p: Complete run.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Hockey puck, from West Point, Army 2 - Cornell 1 can be found in collection 47-7-1958, box 68. MU-125

did.unittitle: Various Awards Banquet Programs annotated with winners, some in a bound volume some signed by players and coaches. Programs, clippings, some photographs. Programs include ECAC championship programs, including 1980 signed by the team. ECAC and NCAA championships programs. Several photographs including Douglas Murray, Sam Paolini and Stephen Baby. Story of the origin of the cowbell cheer by Neil Cohen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably from December 7, 1962.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably from February 9, 1963.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably from February 9, 1963.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably from January 11, 1964.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably at RPI November 26, 1966. Color.

did.unittitle: ESPN Classic, Woody's World broadcast, includes segment about helmets in hockey that has clips of a 1968 Boston University hockey game, broadcast January 15, 2005.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably at Ithaca February 18, 1969

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: the Syracuse Invitational tournament with Colgate on January 3, 1970, at Toronto on January 24, 1970 and the NCAA Final vs Clarkson on March 21, 1970 at Lake Placid.

did.unittitle: Hockey at Cornell by James Maas (2 copies)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Highlights from the 1969 season including the ECAC champiohship trophy presentation. Narrated by Tom Joseph. Also includes bits about the players being students at Cornell, John Hughes talks about being a student, Ken Dryden plays intramural softball.

did.unittitle: 1970 NCAA Championship vs Clarkson ending and celebration
NCAA Hockey Championship highlights against Boston University, digital file only.
Appears to be highlights from the 1977 season

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Magnetic video tape, with broadcast sound. Includes Lance Nethery speaking after winning the Nicky Bawlf Award.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From March 6, 1979. Audio broadcaster is Roy Ives. Newspaper clippings about game included in DVD case.

did.unittitle: 1989 ECAC Championship semifinal vs St. Lawrence
1989 ECAC Championship consolation game vs Harvard
1991 ECAC Championship semifinal vs St. Lawrence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Jack Parker's 500th win celebration.

did.unittitle: Campaign for Big Red Hockey promotional video

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Narrated by Arthur Mintz.

did.unittitle: 1970 NCAA Championship winning team 50th reunion event

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Event held at Cornell Biotechnology Building, emcee is athletic director Andy Noel, includes remarks by President Martha Pollack, talk by Corey Earle on history of Cornell hockey and speech by player John Hughes.

did.unittitle: Cornell Men's Hockey Town Hall April 2, 2020

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes discussions about COVID-19.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Audio cassettes have been digitized and are available on CD-1840 in the collection. The recordings are not complete nor professional, recorded by Fleet Morse.

did.unittitle: ECAC finals consolation game from Boston Garden

dao.xlink:title: ECAC finals consolation game from Boston Garden [Preservation]

daodesc.p: ECAC finals consolation game from Boston Garden [Preservation]

dao.xlink:title: ECAC finals consolation game from Boston Garden [Staff access]

daodesc.p: ECAC finals consolation game from Boston Garden [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes sports news coverage from WHCU, portion of Lou Reycroft interview, portion of Adelphi lacrosse Paul Doherty interview. Pieces of the WHCU radio broadcast of the game starts during the second intermission with an interview with R.P.I coach Mike Addesa. The Cornell Big Red pep band can be clearly heard playing in the background. After the game are postgame interviews and NCAA tournament coverage

dao.xlink:title: 25th Annual Hockey Awards Banquet [Side A] [Staff access]

daodesc.p: 25th Annual Hockey Awards Banquet [Side A] [Staff access]

dao.xlink:title: 25th Annual Hockey Awards Banquet [Side B] [Staff access]

daodesc.p: 25th Annual Hockey Awards Banquet [Side B] [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recorded at The Party House 417 W. State Street. Includes: Side A Lou Reycroft Season recap and player introductions, presentation of year end awards. Other speakers include moderator Bud Hall, Doug Dadswell, Joe Nieuwendyk, Mark Canduro, Dr. Dan Yale. Side B Presentation of awards, speakers include Fleet Morse, Dave Hunter, Dale Coats, Duane Moeser, Wendell Earle, Peter Natyshak, George Taber, Donald Fawcett, Helen Cooley, Laing Kennedy, Doug Dadswell, Mike Schafer Also Cornell lacrosse news coverage including Roy Simmons Jr. and Richie Moran, Syracuse lacrosse postgame coverage versus Syracuse from April 12, 1986. Lastly radio coverage of the hockey award.

did.unittitle: 25th Anniversary celebration of 1970 National Championship Team

dao.xlink:title: 25th Anniversary celebration of 1970 National Championship Team [Preservation]

daodesc.p: 25th Anniversary celebration of 1970 National Championship Team [Preservation]

dao.xlink:title: 25th Anniversary celebration of 1970 National Championship Team [Side B] [Staff access]

daodesc.p: 25th Anniversary celebration of 1970 National Championship Team [Side B] [Staff access]

dao.xlink:title: 25th Anniversary celebration of 1970 National Championship Team [Side A] [Staff access]

daodesc.p: 25th Anniversary celebration of 1970 National Championship Team [Side A] [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A at the St Lawrence game that night, January 27, sound of the pep band. January 28 Roy Ives, Brian McCutcheon talking, audio of 1970 Championship game film highlights against Clarkson until end. Side B Roy Ives, Ned Harkness talking and telling stories about the team. Also part of the Clarkson game that night, can hear Arthur Mintz on the PA. More player stories from members of the 1970 team, unclear who is speaking, ends with singing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Only a portion of the program was recorded. Includes reunion of the 1986 ECAC Championship team, Mike Schafer, player introductions from alumni, Lou Reycroft.

did.unittitle: NCAA East Regional vs Boston College

dao.xlink:title: NCAA East Regional vs Boston College [Preservation]

daodesc.p: NCAA East Regional vs Boston College [Preservation]

dao.xlink:title: NCAA East Regional vs Boston College [Side A] [Staff access]

daodesc.p: NCAA East Regional vs Boston College [Side A] [Staff access]

dao.xlink:title: NCAA East Regional vs Boston College [Side B] [Staff access]

daodesc.p: NCAA East Regional vs Boston College [Side B] [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A, Portions of the WHCU and other news coverage, postgame interviews from March 29 game vs Minnesota Mankato. Side B portions of television broadcast

did.unittitle: 2003 Frozen Four at Buffalo, NY. Game against University of New Hampshire.

dao.xlink:title: 2003 Frozen Four at Buffalo, NY. Game against University of New Hampshire. [Preservation]

daodesc.p: 2003 Frozen Four at Buffalo, NY. Game against University of New Hampshire. [Preservation]

daodesc.p: Frozen Four at Buffalo, NY. Game against University of New Hampshire [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portions of WHCU radio broadcast and other coverage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains news clippings, brochures, and score sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains news clippings, brochures, and score sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains news clippings, brochures, and score sheets

did.unittitle: Documents, photos and videos documenting the history of the program found in the hockey offices and digitized by the hockey program staff. The physical items are retained by the hockey program though in some may have been discarded. These files were not digitized to meet archival standards.
